figures.caption: Fig. 1: Overview of Custom ZeroCLIP. Training (left) optimizes a BERT-LSTM decoder using frozen CLIP embeddings from seen provinces. Inference (right) applies cosine-similarity retrieval to generate captions for unseen provinces without labels.

Fig. 2: Training pipeline of Custom ZeroCLIP. Image and caption tokens are encoded by frozen CLIP, projected into the LM, and optimized using LCE and LCLIP . The BERT encoder, projection layers, and LSTM decoder are trained while CLIP remains frozen.
Fig. 3: Representative samples from seen and unseen provinces used in the inductive zero-shot evaluation protocol [26].
Fig. 4: Training and validation loss curves showing stable convergence without overfitting.
TABLE III: Zero-shot performance on 8 unseen provinces. Bold indicates the best result in each column. † Off-the-shelf evaluation without domain fine-tuning. ‡ Retrieval disabled during inference.

TABLE IV: Qualitative comparison across representative garment categories from seen and unseen provinces. Baseline VLMs generate visually plausible but culturally generic captions, whereas Custom ZeroCLIP recovers province-specific terminology through retrieval-guided zero-shot decoding.
